items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has warned that login credentials for Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways have been exposed, meaning attackers could potentially access your organisation's network. If your workplace uses Fortinet products, IT staff should check for compromised credentials immediately. Separately, a Cloudflare report found that cyberattacks on civil society organisations — including Australian ones — are increasing, so community and not-for-profit groups should be especially alert right now.

A new study also highlights that Australians are embracing AI tools quickly, but trust and safety standards haven't kept pace — a reminder to be cautious about what organisational information you share with AI platforms.

**Practical tip:** Avoid entering sensitive work information, student data, or internal documents into public AI tools unless your organisation has specifically approved them.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC warns of widespread credential exposure affecting critical Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways nationwide.
